cases.title: Registered participant cancels before CancellationDeadline transitions to CancelledByUser
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with CancellationDeadline configured in the future (fixed clock before deadline)
Participant has active Registered registration for the event
No prior cancellation on this registration
steps: Invoke registrationService.cancel(registrationId, participantActorContext) before CancellationDeadline
Query registration row by registrationId
Query Registered seat count for the event
cases.expected: Registration state becomes CancelledByUser with cancelled_at timestamp set; Registered seat count decreases by one; FR-11 satisfied — participant cancelled under allowed deadline conditions per BR-07

cases.title: POST registrations cancel returns CancelledByUser for valid participant cancellation
preconditions: Authenticated participant with Registered registration before CancellationDeadline
Fresh Idempotency-Key header
Known registrationId from prior POST registrations
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel with Authorization bearer and Idempotency-Key
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status with same token
cases.expected: Cancel POST returns HTTP 200 with state CancelledByUser, registrationId, and updatedAt; registration-status reflects same cancelled terminal state; participant successfully cancels via public API

cases.title: Participant can cancel when event has no CancellationDeadline configured
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with CancellationDeadline null or unset
Participant Registered for the event
Fixed clock within registration window
steps: Invoke registrationService.cancel(registrationId, participantActorContext)
Verify registration state and seat release
cases.expected: Cancellation succeeds with CancelledByUser; BR-07 applies only when deadline is configured — absence of deadline does not block participant-initiated cancel

cases.title: Valid cancellation frees seat and promotes top waitlist participant atomically
preconditions: Event at capacity with waitlistEnabled true
Participant A Registered, participant B Waitlisted (FIFO position 1)
Cancellation policy allows A to cancel before deadline
steps: Cancel registration for participant A via registrationService.cancel
Query registration state for A and B in same transaction boundary or immediately after commit
Query Registered count for event
cases.expected: A transitions to CancelledByUser; B transitions from Waitlisted to Registered in same atomic transaction per Workflow C and module contract; Registered count remains at capacity; BR-08 invariant satisfied

cases.title: Flow B cancellation step releases seat and enables waitlist promotion
preconditions: Full event with one Waitlisted participant per Flow B setup
Registered participant authenticated with valid JWT
Cancellation allowed before deadline
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel as Registered participant
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/waitlist for organizer scope or GET registration-status for waitlisted participant B
GET organizer registrations list filtered state=Registered
cases.expected: Cancelling participant shows CancelledByUser; top waitlist participant promoted to Registered; Registered count never exceeds capacity; completes Flow B cancellation segment before check-in step

cases.title: Participant UI cancel registration flow per user Flow 2
preconditions: Participant authenticated and viewing event detail where they are Registered
CancellationDeadline not yet passed
Primary action shows Cancel registration per event-specific component states
steps: Click Cancel registration primary action on event detail
Confirm cancellation if confirmation step is presented
Observe updated registration status on event detail and My Registrations
cases.expected: UI initiates cancellation; status updates to cancelled terminal state with distinct muted token; both event detail and My Registrations reflect cancellation per Flow 2 steps 1–5

cases.title: Cancellation deadline validator permits request before configured deadline
preconditions: Fixed clock abstraction set to timestamp strictly before event CancellationDeadline
Registration in Registered state eligible for cancelByParticipant transition
steps: Call cancellation policy validator with registration context and current clock
Observe validator outcome without persisting
cases.expected: Validator passes without error; cancel pipeline may proceed; aligns with validation catalog BR-07 row and testing plan unit coverage for rule validators

cases.title: Cancellation after CancellationDeadline is rejected with CANCELLATION_DEADLINE_PASSED
preconditions: Event with CancellationDeadline configured in the past relative to fixed clock
Participant still Registered (not yet cancelled)
Event policy blocks late participant self-cancel without approval path
steps: Invoke registrationService.cancel(registrationId, participantActorContext) after deadline
Observe thrown error and unchanged registration state
cases.expected: ApiError with code CANCELLATION_DEADLINE_PASSED and HTTP 422; registration remains Registered; seat not released; FR-11 not satisfied because allowed conditions (before deadline) are not met

cases.title: Late cancellation blocked by event policy returns CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED
preconditions: Event configured with post-deadline policy that blocks participant self-cancel without organizer approval
Fixed clock after CancellationDeadline
Participant Registered
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel with Participant Authorization
Parse error response
cases.expected: HTTP 422 with error.code CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED and user-safe message; registration state unchanged; BR-09 late cancellation policy enforced

cases.title: Duplicate cancel attempt on already cancelled registration returns conflict
preconditions: Participant registration already in CancelledByUser state from prior successful cancel
Fresh Idempotency-Key for second attempt
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel a second time
GET registration-status
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with error.code INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION or equivalent; registration remains CancelledByUser; no duplicate seat release or waitlist side effects

cases.title: Participant cannot cancel another participant registration
preconditions: Participant A Registered for event
Participant B authenticated with valid JWT
Participant B does not own registrationId belonging to A
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationIdOfA}/cancel using Participant B Authorization bearer
cases.expected: HTTP 403 or 404 (out-of-scope hidden); A registration state unchanged Registered; FR-26 own-data scope prevents cross-participant cancellation

cases.title: Unauthenticated cancel request returns 401
preconditions: Valid eventId and registrationId for an existing Registered row
No Authorization header on request
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel without Authorization
cases.expected: HTTP 401 with typed error envelope; registration state unchanged; cancel endpoint requires authenticated session per API auth principles

cases.title: Cancel from CheckedIn registration is rejected as invalid transition
preconditions: Participant registration in CheckedIn state after valid in-window check-in
CancellationDeadline not passed
steps: Invoke registrationService.cancel(registrationId, participantActorContext)
Verify registration state unchanged
cases.expected: Cancellation rejected with HTTP 409 and INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION; state remains CheckedIn; state machine allows cancelByParticipant only from Registered per registration transition table

cases.title: Idempotent cancel retry with same Idempotency-Key returns consistent outcome
preconditions: Participant Registered with valid cancellation policy
First POST cancel succeeded with Idempotency-Key K
steps: Repeat POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel with same Idempotency-Key K
Compare response bodies and registration-status
cases.expected: Retry returns same CancelledByUser snapshot without double seat release or duplicate waitlist promotion; idempotency policy for cancel write endpoint satisfied

cases.title: Concurrent cancellations on full waitlisted event do not double-promote same waitlist slot
preconditions: Event at capacity with multiple Waitlisted participants in FIFO order
Two Registered participants A and B attempt simultaneous valid cancellation
Queue locking enabled per Workflow C guardrail
steps: Trigger concurrent cancel for A and B registrations
After both complete, query states of all waitlisted participants and Registered count
cases.expected: Each cancellation promotes at most one distinct waitlist participant; Registered count never exceeds capacity; no WAITLIST_ORDER_CONFLICT or duplicate promotion of same waitlist entry; FIFO order preserved per BR-06

cases.title: Successful cancel writes traceable status history entry
preconditions: Participant Registered then cancels before deadline
Organizer Admin authorized for status-history endpoint
steps: POST cancel as participant
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?registrationId={registrationId} as Organizer Admin
cases.expected: Status history includes registration.cancelled entry with beforeState Registered and afterState CancelledByUser, registrationId, and occurredAt; supports AC-12 traceability for cancellation transitions

cases.title: Cancellation and waitlist promotion execute in single database transaction
preconditions: Event at capacity with at least one Waitlisted participant
Registered participant eligible to cancel
Integration test harness with transaction inspection or rollback fixture
steps: Simulate failure during waitlist promotion step after seat release attempt
Verify database state after rollback
Execute successful cancel and confirm both transitions commit together
cases.expected: On promotion failure entire transaction rolls back — cancelling participant remains Registered and seat not released; on success both cancel and promotion persist atomically per module breakdown cross-module transaction boundary

cases.title: Cancellation deadline policy behavior covered in integration suite
preconditions: Fixed clock abstraction wired for registration module integration tests
Events with and without CancellationDeadline fixtures seeded
steps: Run integration scenarios: cancel before deadline, on deadline boundary, after deadline
Assert error codes and state outcomes match validation catalog
cases.expected: All deadline scenarios produce deterministic outcomes matching testing plan integration target cancellation deadline policy behavior; no flaky time-dependent failures when clock is controlled

cases.title: Cancellation UI presents clear low-step flow on common viewport sizes
preconditions: Participant Registered on mobile-width and desktop-width viewports
Cancellation allowed by policy
steps: Open event detail on each viewport
Complete cancel registration flow
Verify status feedback and action availability after cancel
cases.expected: Cancel path is reachable in few steps without horizontal scroll or hidden controls; post-cancel UI shows cancelled status token and removes Register/Cancel ambiguity; aligns with registration flow clarity goals for participant self-service
